title: AETHERMOORE Cryptographic Security System
content:
# AETHERMOORE Cryptographic Security System

Project Overview
AETHERMOORE is a unified cryptographic security framework combining spectral coherence-based encryption, topological control-flow integrity, and harmonic verification mechanisms for next-generation secure systems.
Core Components
1. SCBE (Spectral Coherence-Based Encryption)
Phase-breath hyperbolic governance with 14-layer architecture for adaptive authorization and encryption. The mathematical foundation for all geometric security operations.
2. Topological Linearization CFI
Control-flow integrity via Hamiltonian path embeddings in high-dimensional manifolds, enabling zero-runtime-overhead attack detection (90%+ ROP/data-flow detection).
3. Symphonic Cipher SDK
FFT-based harmonic verification for transaction intent validation in Real World Protocol v3. Uses Cooley-Tukey algorithm + Feistel networks for intent modulation.
4. GeoSeal Geometric Trust Manifold
NEW: Dual-space cryptographic binding mapping AI behavior to a sphere (S^n) and policy rules to a hypercube ([0,1]^m). Interior vs exterior paths produce different cryptographic keys and signatures.
5. Langues Weighting System
Six-dimensional exponential weighting metric (KO, AV, RU, CA, UM, DR) for intent-aware cost and trust modeling with golden ratio scaling.
6. Spiralverse Integration
Unified protocol envelope (RWP v2.1) with dual-door consensus, roundtable tiers, and six-language DSL for workflow orchestration.
Strategic Metrics
Uniqueness: 0.98 (98% unique vs. Kyber/Dilithium)
Improvement: 0.28 (28% F1-score gain)
Deployability: 0.99 (226/226 tests, <2ms latency)
Competitive Advantage: 40× combined vs. nearest competitors
Patent Status
USPTO Provisional: Filed
Non-provisional Target: Q4 2026
International (PCT): Planned Q1 2027
